slides.title: Lin Huiyin
slides.subtitle: Rediscover the Architect, Not the Legend
slides.info: 70th Anniversary · Re-examined through work, theory, and action

slides.label: Anniversary
slides.desc: What gets seen is often not her most important part. What truly supports her historical standing is architectural practice, theoretical construction, and disciplinary foundation.

slides.label: Practice
slides.heading: From Tombstone to Station: A Clear Architectural Trajectory

events.title: Liang Qichao Tombstone
events.desc: Starting point upon returning from abroad

items.title: Jihai Railway Station
items.desc: Symbolic expression in landmark public architecture

left.title: Monument to the People's Heroes
points: Proposed expanded pedestal and double-tier steps
Ornamentation and scale reflecting traditional architectural vocabulary

right.title: Babaoshan Revolutionary Cemetery
points: She was ultimately buried here too

slides.label: Theoretical Foundation
slides.heading: In 1932, She Was Already Defining "Chinese Architecture"
points: First systematic discourse on Chinese architecture by a Chinese professional scholar
Rebutted misreadings within Western knowledge frameworks
Proposed key features of the timber frame system
Laid the theoretical skeleton for Chinese architectural history narrative

slides.label: Methodology
slides.heading: What She Truly Left Was a Method

columns.title: Research Method
points: Survey, fieldwork, and historical analysis combined

columns.title: Disciplinary Building
points: Participated in Northeastern and Tsinghua architecture departments

steps.title: Field Survey
steps.desc: Crossing mountains and rivers — not a bystander

steps.title: Professional Mission
steps.desc: "I have so much work to do"

slides.text: To rediscover her is not to return from legend to gossip, but from gossip to profession. What she left is not just works, but a language for how China understands its own architecture.
Seven words on the tombstone — already accurate enough.
